primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup monetary items (dividend receivable) for designation as hedged items in foreign currency risk hedge accounting

primary_authority.reason: Directly governs what items qualify as hedged items for hedge accounting, including the exception for intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements
Establishes the fundamental principle that intragroup monetary items cannot be eliminated without recognizing currency fluctuation effects in consolidated financial statements

supporting_authority.reason: Clarifies scope of foreign currency risk eligible for hedge accounting in net investment hedges and confirms that FX exposure between functional currencies of entities within a group qualifies
Specifies circumstances under which exchange differences on monetary items may be recognized in OCI rather than P&L, relevant to determining hedge accounting treatment

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses cash flow presentation but does not affect the hedge accounting eligibility question
Disclosures about structured entities and financial support are not relevant to hedge accounting eligibility
Addresses measurement of dividend payable, not eligibility of dividend receivable for hedge accounting

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items under the general exclusion (IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 general rule)
Hedge accounting under the intragroup monetary item exception (IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 exception)
Recognition of foreign exchange differences without hedge accounting

approaches.label: Hedge accounting with external party hedged item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 general rule requires hedged items to be with external parties; dividend receivables from external sources (not intragroup) could qualify under this approach

approaches.label: Hedge accounting under intragroup monetary item exception
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 exception permits hedging intragroup monetary items that create uneliminated FX exposure in consolidated financial statements per IAS 21 paragraph 45

approaches.label: Foreign exchange recognition without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 paragraph 45 requires recognition of FX gains/losses on intragroup monetary items in consolidated P&L or OCI; hedge accounting is optional, not mandatory
